calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-06-2025 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-31-2025 Continued To Nov-25-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Quash Service Of Summons; Declaration Of Martin Mclaughlin In Support [Hearing: May 16, 2025, Dept. 301]
Off Calendar May-16-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT MARTIN MCLAUGHLIN's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The unopposed motion to quash service of summons is granted. Specially appearing defendant provides a declaration that he was not personally served and does not match the physical description provided in the proof of service of summons. "When a defendant challenges the court's personal jurisdiction on the ground of improper service of process, the burden is on the plaintiff to prove the existence of jurisdiction by proving, inter alia, the facts requisite to an effective service." (Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 403, 413 [internal quotation marks, citation, and footnote omitted].) Plaintiff presents no evidence to meet that burden.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
